---
title: "Sahil Imtiyaz"
subtitle: "Postdoctoral Researcher,
Basque Center for Applied Mathematics (BCAM), Bilbao, Spain"
about:
  template: solana
  image: image.jpg
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:simtiyaz@bcamath.org
    - icon: file-earmark-person
      text: CV
      href: cv.qmd
    - icon: person-lines-fill
      text: Contact
      href: contact.qmd
page-layout: full
---

## Mathematical Structures, Dynamics, and Computation in Complex Systems

**Quantum foundations · Quantum information and computation · Topological-geometric methods · Evolving networks and communication systems · Mathematical and theoretical biology**

I study complex systems in which structure, dynamics, information, and constraints interact across scales. My work develops mathematical and computational frameworks for understanding how local interactions give rise to coherent system-level behaviour, how contextual and geometric structure shapes what a system can do, and how evolving networks, quantum models, and biological systems can be analysed through common structural principles.

---

## Research at a Glance

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Quantum Foundations

Non-locality, contextuality, Bell–Kochen–Specker models, and philosophical questions in quantum theory, studied through topological, geometric, algebraic, and sheaf/bundle methods.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Quantum Information and Computation

Mathematical structures behind quantum advantage, contextuality as a computational resource, and structural approaches to quantum information, computation, and cognition.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Topological, Geometric, and Algebraic Methods

Algebraic topology, sheaf theory, fibre bundles, obstruction theory, discrete Morse theory, category-theoretic ideas, and gauge/holonomy methods for local-to-global structure.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Evolving Networks and Communication Systems

Evolving, heterogeneous, and reconfigurable networked systems, with emphasis on dynamic feasibility, partial information, communication infrastructures, and structural diagnostics.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Mathematical and Theoretical Biology

Mathematical and theoretical models of multiscale biological processes, biological communication, circular causality, active interfaces, and adaptive ecological systems.
:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Featured Work

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Bundle/Gauge-Theoretic Structure of Quantum Contextuality

I study quantum contextuality and non-locality through bundle-theoretic, gauge-theoretic, topological, and algebraic structures. This work investigates Bell–Kochen–Specker models, local-to-global obstruction, underlying symmetry principles, loop/interface holonomy, and the geometric foundations of quantum contextuality in quantum theory.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Topology-Driven Computation and Quantum Advantage

I am interested in the mathematical structures behind quantum advantage and the role of contextuality as a computational resource. More broadly, I study topology-driven models of computation in which structure, constraints, and computation interact: local computational processes are shaped by global organisation, while computation can in turn modify the topology, invariants, or observable structure of the system. This direction is motivated by the search for structural foundations of quantum algorithms and new forms of computation over changing spaces.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### The Evolving Reconfigurable Network Bundle

**A Geometric Framework for Evolving Communication Systems**

I develop geometric frameworks for evolving, heterogeneous, and reconfigurable networked systems. In this work, dynamic feasibility is studied as a structural problem: when can local configurations, paths, policies, or updates be assembled into globally coherent behaviour under changing network constraints? The framework is aimed at communication infrastructures where topology, configuration spaces, partial information, and admissible transformations evolve together.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### Constraint Semantics and Cross-Scale Biological Communication

I develop structural semantics for multi-level biological systems, where molecular, membrane, cellular, tissue, and ecological processes interact through active interfaces. This work includes cross-scale biological communication, Hodgkin-type signalling loops, cardiac excitation–conduction, circular causality, and the distinction between pathway closure and recurrence in one fixed state space.
:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Explore

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### [Research](research.qmd)

Broader research programme, methods, and current directions.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### [Publications](publications.qmd)

Published papers, preprints, submitted manuscripts, and thesis work.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### [CV](cv.qmd)

Academic CV and professional profile.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### [Contact](contact.qmd)

Email, affiliation, and academic links.
:::
:::
:::

:::
